content.problemStatement:
A (111-indexed) binary string SSS of length NNN is called a xor palindrome if the value of Si⊕S(N+1−i)S_i \oplus S_{(N + 1 - i)}Si​⊕S(N+1−i)​ is the same for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.

For example, 000, 111111111111 and 010101010101 are xor palindromes, while 111011101110 and 110101110101110101 are not.

You are given a binary string SSS of length NNN. Determine if it is possible to rearrange it to form a xor palindrome or not.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN — the length of the binary string SSS.
The second line of each test case contains the binary string SSS containing 000s and 111s only.


Output Format
For each test case, output YES\texttt{YES}YES if it is possible to rearrange SSS to convert it into a xor palindrome. Otherwise output NO\texttt{NO}NO.


You may print each character of YES\texttt{YES}YES and NO\texttt{NO}NO in uppercase or lowercase (for example, yes\texttt{yes}yes, yEs\texttt{yEs}yEs, Yes\texttt{Yes}Yes will be considered identical).
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Then T test cases follow. Each test case consists of two lines: the first line contains an integer N – the length of the binary string. The second line contains a binary string S of length N consisting only of characters '0' and '1'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "YES" if the characters of S can be rearranged to form a xor palindrome, otherwise output "NO".

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Test case 1: "00" already satisfies xor = 0 for the only pair.
Test case 2: Rearrange to "0110" gives xor = 1 for every opposite pair.
Test case 3: Rearrange to "010" (a palindrome) gives xor = 0.
Test case 4: No arrangement yields a constant xor for all pairs.
Length is odd, any palindrome works; the given string is already a palindrome, so xor = 0 for all pairs.
Length 1 always works. For length 2, counts are equal so xor = 1 arrangement exists. For length 6, counts are equal (3 zeros, 3 ones) → xor = 1 arrangement exists.

hidden.label: tricky even length, both counts odd and unequal

solution.approach: For a string of length N let cnt0 be the number of '0's and cnt1 the number of '1's. If N is odd the middle character pairs with itself, so the required xor for every opposite pair must be 0. This forces the string to be a palindrome, which is always possible for odd N because exactly one of cnt0 or cnt1 is odd. Hence answer is always YES for odd N. For even N the xor of every opposite pair must be either all 0 or all 1. *All 0* means each opposite pair contains equal bits, i.e. the whole string is a palindrome – this is possible iff both cnt0 and cnt1 are even. *All 1* means each opposite pair contains different bits, i.e. each pair consumes one '0' and one '1' – this is possible iff cnt0 == cnt1. Therefore the answer is YES if N is odd, or cnt0==cnt1, or (cnt0%2==0 && cnt1%2==0). Otherwise NO. The algorithm just counts zeros, derives ones, and checks the three conditions – O(N) per test case, O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(N) per test case, O(total N) over all test cases

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T,N;static char S[100005];if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1)return 0;while(T--){scanf("%d",&N);scanf("%s",S);int cnt0=0,cnt1=0;for(int i=0;i<N;++i){if(S[i]=='0')cnt0++;else cnt1++;}int ok=0;if(N%2==1)ok=1;else if(cnt0==cnt1)ok=1;else if((cnt0%2==0)&&(cnt1%2==0))ok=1;printf("%s\n",ok?"YES":"NO");}return 0;}
code.Python3:
import sys
def solve():
    data=sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it=iter(data)
    T=int(next(it))
    out=[]
    for _ in range(T):
        N=int(next(it))
        S=next(it)
        cnt0=S.count('0')
        cnt1=N-cnt0
        if N%2==1:
            ok=True
        else:
            ok = (cnt0==cnt1) or ((cnt0%2==0) and (cnt1%2==0))
        out.append('YES' if ok else 'NO')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))
if __name__=='__main__':
    solve()
